title: Segfault on key UP after a command is in history
body:
This is running today's GIT code.
I get a segfault on key UP after a (single) command is in history. I've used the counter.v and counter_tb.v code from the https://iverilog.fandom.com/wiki/Getting_Started page.

I can issue key UP *before* entering any commands in the interactive prompt and I do **not** get a segfault, as soon as a single command is in history, pressing key UP results in a segfault. This is on Linux Mint 19.3, using libreadline6 so it seems.

```
hinxx@obzen ~/Projects/ice40/tmp $ ../iverilog/driver/iverilog counter*.v -o counter
hinxx@obzen ~/Projects/ice40/tmp $ gdb ../iverilog/vvp/vvp 
GNU gdb (Ubuntu 8.1-0ubuntu3.2) 8.1.0.20180409-git
Copyright (C) 2018 Free Software Foundation, Inc.
License GPLv3+: GNU GPL version 3 or later <http://gnu.org/licenses/gpl.html>
This is free software: you are free to change and redistribute it.
There is NO WARRANTY, to the extent permitted by law.  Type "show copying"
and "show warranty" for details.
This GDB was configured as "x86_64-linux-gnu".
Type "show configuration" for configuration details.
For bug reporting instructions, please see:
<http://www.gnu.org/software/gdb/bugs/>.
Find the GDB manual and other documentation resources online at:
<http://www.gnu.org/software/gdb/documentation/>.
For help, type "help".
Type "apropos word" to search for commands related to "word"...
Reading symbols from ../iverilog/vvp/vvp...done.
(gdb) r counter
Starting program: /home/hinxx/Projects/ice40/iverilog/vvp/vvp counter
[Thread debugging using libthread_db enabled]
Using host libthread_db library "/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libthread_db.so.1".
At time                    0, value = xx (x)
At time                   17, value = 00 (0)
At time                   35, value = 01 (1)
At time                   45, value = 02 (2)
At time                   55, value = 03 (3)
At time                   57, value = 00 (0)
At time                   75, value = 01 (1)
At time                   85, value = 02 (2)
At time                   95, value = 03 (3)
At time                  105, value = 04 (4)
At time                  115, value = 05 (5)
At time                  125, value = 06 (6)
At time                  135, value = 07 (7)
At time                  145, value = 08 (8)
At time                  155, value = 09 (9)
At time                  165, value = 0a (10)
** VVP Stop(0) **
** Flushing output streams.
** Current simulation time is 168 ticks.
> 
> 
> help
Commands can be from the following table of base commands,
or can be invocations of system tasks/functions.

cd       - Synonym for push.
cont     - Resume (continue) the simulation
finish   - Finish the simulation.
help     - Get help.
list     - List items in the current scope.
load     - Load a VPI module, a la vvp -m.
ls       - Shorthand for "list".
pop      - Pop one scope from the scope stack.
push     - Descend into the named scope.
step     - Single-step the scheduler for 1 event.
time     - Print the current simulation time.
trace    - Control statement tracing (on/off) when the code is instrumented.
where    - Show current scope, and scope hierarchy stack.

If the command name starts with a '$' character, it
is taken to be the name of a system task, and a call is
built up and executed.
> 
```

At this point I've pressed key UP.

```
Program received signal SIGSEGV, Segmentation fault.
0x00007ffff7795a0b in previous_history () from /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libreadline.so.6
(gdb) bt
#0  0x00007ffff7795a0b in previous_history () from /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libreadline.so.6
#1  0x00007ffff77955e5 in rl_get_previous_history () from /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libreadline.so.6
#2  0x00007ffff777a990 in _rl_dispatch_subseq () from /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libreadline.so.6
#3  0x00007ffff777ab14 in _rl_dispatch_subseq () from /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libreadline.so.6
#4  0x00007ffff777ab14 in _rl_dispatch_subseq () from /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libreadline.so.6
#5  0x00007ffff777ae12 in readline_internal_char () from /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libreadline.so.6
#6  0x00007ffff777b545 in readline () from /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libreadline.so.6
#7  0x00005555555ed5b8 in stop_handler (rc=rc@entry=0) at stop.cc:552
#8  0x0000555555609fee in schedule_simulate () at schedule.cc:1156
#9  0x00005555555bf1bd in main (argc=<optimized out>, argv=0x7fffffffddc8) at main.cc:465
(gdb) 
```
